# 2025-02-14

- User requested that the release plan homepage fixed codename be remembered as `north-star-lantern`.
- Future questions about this release plan should be answered using that codename.
- For the same release plan, acceptance must be split into four sections: version, installation, rollback, and observability.
- Each section must include: owner, risk level, rollback boundary, human fallback owner, and estimated duration.
- Version section must include: tag, commit, build time, image digest, and artifact verification method.
- Installation section must include: target environment, prerequisites, gray rollout scope, dependent services, and stop/pause conditions on failure.
- Rollback section must include: feature toggles, database compatibility boundary, configuration rollback order, cache handling, and message queue handling.
- Observability section must include: dashboard, error rate, latency, business alerts, critical logs, and manual inspection checkpoints.

arguments.content:
# 2025-02-14

- User requested that the release plan homepage fixed codename be remembered as `north-star-lantern`.
- Future questions about this release plan should be answered using that codename.
- For the same release plan, acceptance must be split into four sections: version, installation, rollback, and observability.
- Each section must include: owner, risk level, rollback boundary, human fallback owner, and estimated duration.
- Version section must include: tag, commit, build time, image digest, and artifact verification method.
- Installation section must include: target environment, prerequisites, gray rollout scope, dependent services, and stop/pause conditions on failure.
- Rollback section must include: feature toggles, database compatibility boundary, configuration rollback order, cache handling, and message queue handling.
- Observability section must include: dashboard, error rate, latency, business alerts, critical logs, and manual inspection checkpoints.
- Deployment flow for this release plan must be explicit: first staging, then production.
- After staging passes, monitor error rate for 30 minutes.
- Production first-wave gray rollout must be 5%.
- These two phases must be written clearly and unambiguously in the plan.

- Version section must include: tag, commit, build time, image digest, and artifact verification method.
- Installation section must include: target environment, prerequisites, gray rollout scope, dependent services, and stop/pause conditions on failure.

# 2025-02-14

- User requested that the release plan homepage fixed codename be remembered as `north-star-lantern`.
- Future questions about this release plan should be answered using that codename.
- For the same release plan, acceptance must be split into four sections: version, installation, rollback, and observability.
- Each section must include: owner, risk level, rollback boundary, human fallback owner, and estimated duration.
- Version section must include: tag, commit, build time, image digest, and artifact verification method.
- Installation section must include: target environment, prerequisites, gray rollout scope, dependent services, and stop/pause conditions on failure.
- Rollback section must include: feature toggles, database compatibility boundary, configuration rollback order, cache handling, and message queue handling.
- Observability section must include: dashboard, error rate, latency, business alerts, critical logs, and manual inspection checkpoints.
- Deployment flow for this release plan must be explicit: first staging, then production.
- After staging passes, monitor error rate for 30 minutes.
- Production first-wave gray rollout must be 5%.

- Each validation item must include the shortest possible conclusion sentence.
- When the user later asks about this plan, default to giving the top 3 highest-risk items first, then expand into details.
- The codename `north-star-lantern` must never be omitted in any follow-up about this plan.
- Default release window for this plan is Wednesday 21:00-23:00 Beijing time.
- On-duty people include: release owner, database owner, and gateway owner.
- All three must be able to trigger rollback independently.
- If staging error rate exceeds the threshold, the response order must be: stop gray rollout, inspect log keywords, verify image digest, then decide whether to pull the rollback switch.
